Saccharum officinarum, commonly known as sugarcane, is a tropical plant native to Southeast Asia that belongs to the family Poaceae. The juice of sugarcane contains various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. The bioactive constituents of sugarcane include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and polysaccharides, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with sugarcane are juice, syrup, and extracts, often used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions.
